facts.label: BORN FROM THE FLAG
facts.story: Bulgaria's white shirt, green shorts, and red socks trace directly to the national flag, giving the team its second nickname — Trikolyorite, the Tricolors — alongside 'The Lions,' a nod to the beasts guarding the national coat of arms. The federation ran its own kit production until 1974, when Adidas took over: a partnership that lasted twenty years, through the golden generation's 1994 run, before Puma became supplier in 1995, with the tricolor identity carried into the new era.

facts.label: FIVE CUPS, ZERO WINS
facts.story: Bulgaria reached five World Cups — 1962, 1966, 1970, 1974, and 1986 — without winning a single match, drawing six and losing ten across sixteen games. The breakthrough finally came in 1994, sparked by Emil Kostadinov's stoppage-time strike in Paris that eliminated France from qualifying. Once at the tournament, Bulgaria beat Greece and Argentina before knocking out Germany, turning two decades of World Cup futility into a run to the semi-finals.

1962.headline: No Wins in Chile
1962.homeColors: white shirt, green shorts, red socks

1986.headline: Advancing Without a Win

annotations.label: THROUGH WITHOUT A WIN
annotations.story: Two draws and a defeat to Argentina left Bulgaria third in their group without a single win, but a favorable record among third-placed teams pushed them into the knockout round anyway. Bulgaria and Uruguay became the first nations in World Cup history to reach the second round without winning a group match.

1986.homeColors: white shirt, green shorts, red socks (Adidas)

1994.headline: Golden Generation's Historic Run

annotations.label: LETCHKOV'S HEADER STUNS GERMANY
annotations.story: Down 1–0 to reigning champions Germany after a Lothar Matthäus penalty, Hristo Stoichkov leveled with a 75th-minute free kick before Yordan Letchkov rose for a diving header three minutes later to complete the upset. Sofia's streets filled with celebrating fans that night — the biggest win in Bulgarian football history.

1994.homeColors: white shirt with green diagonal side stripes, green shorts, red trim (Adidas)
